intro.title: What is Spaced Repetition System (SRS)?
intro.text: The Spaced Repetition System (SRS) is a scientifically proven learning methodology that optimizes the transfer of information to long-term memory. It is based on Hermann Ebbinghaus's "forgetting curve" research.

forgettingCurve.title: The Forgetting Curve
forgettingCurve.text: Ebbinghaus discovered that newly learned information is quickly forgotten if not reviewed. However, strategic reviews at specific intervals dramatically increase retention.

leitner.title: Leitner System
leitner.text: Developed by Sebastian Leitner in the 1970s, this system organizes flashcards into different boxes based on difficulty level.

algorithm.title: FinTechTerms Algorithm
algorithm.text: Our application enhances the Leitner system with the SuperMemo-2 algorithm for a smarter learning experience.

rules.title: Correct Answer
rules.desc: Card moves to the next box

academic.title: Academic Foundation
references: Ebbinghaus, H. (1885). Memory: A Contribution to Experimental Psychology
Leitner, S. (1972). So lernt man lernen
Wozniak, P. & Gorzelanczyk, E. (1994). Optimization of Repetition Spacing

benefits.title: Benefits of SRS
items: Reduces learning time by up to 50%

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The requested section does not exist in the current catalog structure or has been moved to another research surface.

aboutProject.abstractText: FinTechTerms is a specialized Learning Management System (LMS) designed to accelerate financial terminology acquisition. The project utilizes Spaced Repetition Algorithms (SRS) to reduce cognitive load in trilingual (TR-EN-RU) lexical learning within the FinTech domain.
